feed.title: Salesforce ties executive compensation to ESG goals
feed.summary: Salesforce announced that a portion of executive variable pay for those at the executive vice president level and above will be tied to ESG measures, starting this fiscal year. These measures will focus on increasing representation for diverse employees and reducing air travel emissions, as well as increasing spend with suppliers signed onto Salesforce's Sustainability Exhibit. This move reflects a growing trend among major companies to link executive compensation to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) and sustainability goals.

feed.title: Apple Readies New MacBooks and iMacs for Part Three of Overhaul
feed.summary: Apple is preparing for the third phase of its Mac overhaul, with new MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, and iMac models expected in 2022. This article from Mark Gurman highlights Apple's upcoming product launch event, focusing on its Mac plans for the year. It also briefly mentions changes at Peloton and the development of iOS 16.

feed.title: Trump's new social media application 'Truth Social' begins gradual rollout
feed.summary: Donald Trump's "Truth Social" app began a gradual rollout on Sunday, with the aim of being fully operational in the US by the end of March. The platform is intended as an alternative to major social media sites that banned Trump after the January 6, 2021 Capitol assault. Former congressman Devin Nunes, CEO of Trump Media & Technology Group, stated that the app would be available on the Apple App Store, celebrating the return of free speech for those who felt their voices were canceled.

feed.title: Truth Social backed by Trump Media and Technology group launches
feed.summary: Former U.S. President Donald Trump's new social media app, Truth Social, officially launched on Monday, February 21, 2022. The app, backed by the Trump Media and Technology group, positions itself as a competitor to existing platforms like Facebook and Twitter. This launch comes over a year after Trump was suspended from major social media sites following the January 6 Capitol riot.

feed.title: Kraft Heinz taps Google for multiyear transformation push
feed.summary: Kraft Heinz announced a multiyear strategic partnership with Google to accelerate its digital transformation, leveraging Google Cloud's AI and ML to drive food innovations, build a customer data platform, and improve media efficiency. This initiative is part of Kraft Heinz's broader strategy to reinvest in its brands and achieve cost savings by 2024, enabling data-driven decisions as consumer behavior shifts online. The partnership aims to enhance personalization at scale, optimize media activations, and address changing market challenges amid the pandemic.

feed.title: Apple Al Maryah Island opens Friday in the heart of Abu Dhabi
feed.summary: Apple previewed its new retail destination, Apple Al Maryah Island, in Abu Dhabi, set to open on Friday, February 25, 2022. The store is elevated above cascading water, creating a direct connection to the water's edge, and offers unique design elements, including ramps made of Absolute Black granite and a golden carbon fiber roof. It will host special "Today at Apple" sessions inspired by Abu Dhabi's creative culture, provide business advice, and feature a multicultural team speaking over 30 languages.

feed.title: Life360 shares plunge -30% as losses accelerate to fuel growth
feed.summary: Life360 (ASX: 360) experienced a nearly 30% drop in its share price after reporting a larger-than-expected net loss of -US$33.6 million for 2021, despite revenue increasing by 40%. The company attributed accelerating losses to increased operating expenses in research, sales, and administration, aimed at fueling growth. Investors reacted negatively to the growing losses and concerns surfaced regarding Apple's AirTags privacy issues potentially impacting Life360's Tile acquisition.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway posts higher profit, extends buybacks
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. reported a significant increase in fourth-quarter profit, driven by improved operating results and investment gains. The company's operating income surged by 45% to $7.29 billion, while net income rose 11% to $39.65 billion, greatly influenced by its holdings, particularly in Apple Inc. Berkshire Hathaway also continued its share buybacks, repurchasing an additional $6.9 billion in stock during the quarter.

feed.title: Warren Buffett Takes ‘Mildly Attractive’ Path With $27 Billion Buybacks
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway repurchased a record $27.1 billion in stock in 2021, a strategy Buffett called "mildly attractive" given the lack of appealing acquisition opportunities due to high valuations. The buybacks helped reduce Berkshire's substantial cash pile. Buffett also highlighted Apple as a key holding and praised its CEO, Tim Cook, while Berkshire reported strong fourth-quarter earnings.

feed.title: Apple Halts Product Sales in Russia After Ukraine Invasion
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has ceased all product sales in Russia and removed RT News and Sputnik News apps from its App Stores outside of Russia, citing deep concern over the invasion of Ukraine. The company also disabled traffic and live-incident features in Ukraine for safety reasons. This decision aligns Apple with other global companies taking action against Russia.

feed.title: Google, Meta face penalties in Russia as deadline passes to open local offices
feed.summary: Google and Meta are among several internet companies facing potential penalties in Russia for failing to establish local offices and comply with a new communications law. Russia's state communications regulator, Roskomnadzor, had set a deadline of the end of February for these companies to open local offices or face restrictions. The move is part of Russia's broader effort to control internet content, with fines previously issued for failing to censor anti-government content, and recent curtailments of access to platforms like Facebook in retaliation for censoring state media.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE Ford set to announce plans to run EV, ICE as separate businesses -sources
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. plans to announce a reorganization to run its electric vehicle (EV) and internal-combustion engine (ICE) units as separate businesses. This strategic move aims to accelerate EV growth and potentially allow for separate financial reporting down the line, although CEO Jim Farley has stated there are currently no plans for a full spinoff. The company seeks to improve profit margins in both divisions by cutting costs in ICE and investing more in EV development.

feed.title: Apple, Netflix, TikTok strike back against Russian state media content
feed.summary: Several major tech and entertainment companies, including Apple, Netflix, and TikTok, are taking action against Russian state media and suspending services in Russia following the invasion of Ukraine. This includes Netflix refusing to carry Russian federal channels, Apple pausing product sales and limiting services, and social media platforms restricting access to and demoting content from outlets like RT and Sputnik due to concerns about propaganda and disinformation. The moves reflect a global condemnation of Russia's actions and a united front from U.S. businesses.

feed.title: 3 Stocks to Buy to Get a Boost From Trump’s Truth Social
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump's new social media platform, Truth Social, has launched and is gaining initial popularity, creating investment opportunities. This article highlights three stocks positioned to benefit from Truth Social's growth: Digital World Acquisition Corp. (DWAC) as the SPAC behind the platform, CF Acquisition VI (CFVI) due to its connection with the content platform Rumble, and Palo Alto Networks (PANW) for its critical cybersecurity services essential to platform security. While highlighting significant upside potential, the author also notes associated risks and regulatory hurdles.

feed.title: Exxon to exit Russia, leaving $4 bln in assets
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil announced its decision to exit Russian oil and gas operations and halt new investments due to Moscow's invasion of Ukraine, impacting over $4 billion in assets and putting a proposed LNG facility on Sakhalin Island in doubt. This move follows similar actions by other Western companies, though Exxon's Russian business is considered relatively small within its wider enterprise. The company had been under pressure to sever ties and is now evacuating U.S. employees from the region.

feed.title: Apple to host spring event next week, low-cost 5G iPhone in focus
feed.summary: Apple Inc. announced it will host its annual spring product launch event on March 8, where it is expected to unveil a low-cost 5G iPhone, a new iPad Air, and a high-end Mac Mini. The new iPhone SE model is rumored to feature an improved camera and faster processor, continuing Apple's focus on 5G capabilities. This event follows a period where Apple successfully navigated supply chain issues to achieve record sales.

feed.title: Crypto exchanges won't bar Russians, raising fears of sanctions backdoor
feed.summary: Major cryptocurrency exchanges are refusing to implement a blanket ban on Russian users, despite Western sanctions targeting Moscow and a plea from the Ukrainian government. They argue that a full ban goes against the libertarian ethos of cryptocurrency, though they will block accounts of individuals explicitly targeted by sanctions. This stance raises concerns among anti-money laundering specialists that crypto could serve as a loophole for Russians to circumvent financial isolation.

feed.title: Ikea and the owner of T.J. Maxx are the latest retailers to halt Russia operations.
feed.summary: Ikea and TJX, parent company of T.J. Maxx and Marshalls, have joined a growing list of Western retailers halting operations in Russia due to the invasion of Ukraine. Ikea is pausing all exports, imports, production, and retail in Russia and Belarus, affecting 15,000 employees, while TJX is divesting its 25% stake in the Russian off-price retailer Familia. These actions align with many other European and American businesses ceasing sales and operations, making donations, and expressing support for the Ukrainian people.

feed.title: Amid Ukraine fallout, crisis-hardened chipmakers move to adapt
feed.summary: Semiconductor manufacturers are adapting to potential disruptions from the Russia-Ukraine conflict by stockpiling materials, diversifying suppliers, and leveraging previous lessons from geopolitical events like Russia's annexation of Crimea. While Russia itself is a small market for chips, the war's broader impact on electronics consumption and supply chain stability for key materials like neon gas is a concern, though the industry has shown resilience. The conflict highlights the need for strategic reshoring and decoupling from hostile states to build greater industry robustness against future crises.

feed.title: First U.S. refiner suspends purchases of Russian crude for Hawaii refinery
feed.summary: Par Pacific Holdings Inc. has announced it will suspend purchases of Russian crude oil for its Hawaii refinery, becoming the first U.S. refiner to publicly do so after sanctions imposed due to Russia's invasion of Ukraine. The company plans to source crude from North and South America and was the fifth largest U.S. importer of Russian crude in 2021. This move comes as other Western companies halt Russian operations and the U.S. considers further sanctions on Russia's oil sector.

feed.title: Trump hasn’t posted on Truth Social in weeks as waitlist swells
feed.summary: Donald Trump has not posted on Truth Social for over two weeks, despite the platform's waitlist reportedly swelling to over a million users. The app, which launched in February, quickly rose to the top of Apple's free apps chart but has since fallen to 90th place as many users remain stuck on the waitlist. Despite these operational hiccups, the parent company, Trump Media and Technology Group, is set to go public through a merger with Digital World Acquisition Corp., whose shares have surged significantly since the merger announcement.

feed.title: DXC Is Pulling Out Of Russia, Condemns ‘Unprovoked Attack On Ukraine’
feed.summary: DXC Technology announced its exit from the Russian market in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine, impacting approximately 4,000 employees. The company condemned the "unprovoked attack" and stated it would no longer pursue business in Russia, while also providing support to its employees in the region, including financial assistance and relocation support. This move follows similar actions by other major IT firms and has raised concerns among solution providers about potential increases in cyberattacks.

feed.title: Ukraine war tests whether marketers can address crisis meaningfully
feed.summary: The war in Ukraine is challenging marketers to address crises in a meaningful way, with consumers expecting concrete action from brands rather than just statements or paused marketing. A Gartner survey reveals that an overwhelming majority of consumers want brands to reconsider doing business in Russia and ensure the safety of employees, emphasizing a shift towards corporate governance and supply chain operations. While traditional communication tactics like public statements rank low in consumer preference, marketers must strategically plan for a time when consumer-facing messaging will be more receptive, keeping a close eye on the rapidly evolving situation.

feed.title: Sonos Roam SL vs Sonos Roam: which Bluetooth speaker will be the best?
feed.summary: This article compares the Sonos Roam SL and the Sonos Roam, two portable Bluetooth speakers from Sonos, to help consumers decide which one best suits their needs. The key difference lies in the Roam having a built-in microphone for voice controls, Auto Trueplay, and Sound Swap, features absent from the more affordable Roam SL. Both speakers share the same design, battery life, and core audio technology, making the choice dependent on budget and the importance of smart features.

feed.title: Microsoft closes on $16 billion acquisition of Nuance
feed.summary: Microsoft has completed its $16 billion acquisition of speech recognition company Nuance Communications. This deal, Microsoft's second-largest, aims to expand its presence in the healthcare industry through Nuance's medical dictation and transcription tools. The acquisition received clearance from British and European Union antitrust regulators after initial scrutiny.

feed.title: EPAM Shares Continue to Fall on Ukraine Crisis & Russia Exit
feed.summary: EPAM Systems' shares fell significantly due to the escalating Russia-Ukraine crisis and the company's decision to exit Russia. EPAM, with substantial exposure in Eastern Europe, announced it would discontinue services in Russia and offer humanitarian aid to Ukrainian employees, aligning with U.S. sanctions. The conflict is expected to impact the company's revenues and profitability due to its large workforce in the affected regions.

feed.title: Mac Studio - Technical Specifications
feed.summary: This document provides detailed technical specifications for the Apple Mac Studio, outlining two models with either the Apple M4 Max chip or the Apple M3 Ultra chip. It covers key features such as CPU and GPU cores, Neural Engine, memory and storage configurations, display support, connectivity ports, physical dimensions, and environmental considerations. The document also highlights the device's operating system (macOS), built-in accessibility features, and bundled applications.

feed.title: Apple unveils M1 Ultra, the world’s most powerful chip for a personal computer
feed.summary: Apple has announced M1 Ultra, its new chip for personal computers, which is available in the new Mac Studio. The M1 Ultra utilizes Apple's UltraFusion packaging architecture to combine two M1 Max chips, resulting in 114 billion transistors, a 20-core CPU, a 64-core GPU, and a 32-core Neural Engine. This powerful chip offers unprecedented performance and power efficiency for demanding tasks like 3D rendering and video editing, while consuming significantly less energy than high-end PC desktops.

feed.title: AMD Radeon™ PRO W6600X GPU for Mac Pro Unleashes New Creative Possibilities for Design and Content Creation Professionals
feed.summary: AMD has announced the availability of the new AMD Radeon™ PRO W6600X GPU for Mac Pro, aimed at creative professionals. Built on the AMD RDNA™ 2 architecture, it features AMD Infinity Cache™ and GDDR6 memory, offering high performance and visual fidelity for demanding professional applications. This GPU completes the AMD Radeon PRO W6000X series, providing a mid-range solution for content creation needs.

feed.title: Super-app, moomoo, is the only investment platform to break through top five most downloaded finance apps in Singapore in 2021 since its launch - PR Newswire APAC
feed.summary: Moomoo, an investment super-app, has become the only investment platform to rank among the top five most downloaded finance apps in Singapore's Apple App Store and Google Play Store in 2021 since its launch on March 8, 2021. The platform, a subsidiary of Nasdaq-listed Futu Holdings, also announced its official debut in Australia on March 8, 2022. Moomoo has rapidly expanded in Singapore, acquiring 220,000 registered users and 100,000 paying clients within its first three months, and has obtained approvals-in-principle for all SGX memberships, aiming to be a full-service digital investment platform.

feed.title: Intel's $20 billion investment in Ohio promises thousands of jobs, but can the state and company deliver?
feed.summary: Intel's $20 billion investment in Ohio, set to create 3,000 permanent jobs and 7,000 construction jobs, faces scrutiny regarding its long-term success. While the state offers over $2 billion in incentives, experts question Intel's ability to maintain its competitive edge and Ohio's capacity to sustain the promised economic benefits, drawing comparisons to past manufacturing ventures in the state. Intel plans to address this by investing in talent development and implementing a new strategy to regain leadership in chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Spotify and Discord are down for some users
feed.summary: Spotify and Discord experienced major service disruptions on Tuesday, March 8, 2022, leading to users being logged out and messaging issues. Downdetector reported problems starting around 12:49 p.m. EST for Spotify, while Discord experienced similar outages at the same time. Both companies worked to resolve the issues, with services gradually returning online in the afternoon.

feed.title: Our Ultimate Stock-Pickers' Top 10 Buys and Sells
feed.summary: This article identifies the top 10 high-conviction buys and sells among Morningstar's Ultimate Stock-Pickers for the fourth quarter of 2021, based on an aggregate analysis of their holdings. It highlights key companies like Microsoft and 3M as top buys and Marsh & McLennan as a prominent sell, detailing the Morningstar analysts' fair value estimates, economic moats, and investment theses for these firms. The report assists investors by providing insights into attractive investment ideas and valuation considerations.

feed.title: Amazon announces 20-for-1 stock split, $10 bln share buyback
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has announced a 20-for-1 stock split and authorized a $10 billion share buyback plan, causing its shares to rise by 7% in extended trading. This marks Amazon's first stock split since 1999, aimed at making shares more accessible to investors and increasing flexibility for employees managing equity. Trading with the new share price will commence on June 6.

feed.title: Peloton now lets you use Apple Watch as a heart rate monitor, no GymKit required
feed.summary: Peloton has introduced a new feature allowing Bike and Tread users to connect their Apple Watch for heart rate monitoring, eliminating the need for GymKit or separate heart rate monitors. This integration enables the use of the Apple Watch's heart rate data to calculate Peloton's Strive Score. Users need to ensure the Peloton app is on both their iPhone and Apple Watch and then follow a setup process involving the Health app to establish the connection.

feed.title: Why Nvidia sees a future in software and services: Recurring revenue
feed.summary: Nvidia is shifting its business model to focus on software and services to generate recurring revenue, moving beyond its traditional hardware sales. CFO Colette Kress highlighted opportunities in autonomous vehicles, where Nvidia's software will be monetized over the life of the car, and the metaverse, envisioning annual revenues of $1,000 per creative user and per digital twin bot. This strategic pivot aims to create more predictable income streams, leveraging existing hardware with new software offerings and expanding into new markets.

feed.title: Amazon (AMZN) Approves 20-for-1 Stock Split, $10B Buyback
feed.summary: Amazon's board of directors approved a 20-for-1 stock split and a $10 billion share buyback. This marks Amazon's first stock split since 1999 and is intended to make shares more accessible to a broader range of investors and employees. Other major tech companies like Apple, Tesla, and Alphabet have also undertaken stock splits recently.

feed.title: Traction on Demand’s Greg Malpass on the Salesforce offer he couldn’t refuse
feed.summary: Greg Malpass, founder and CEO of Traction on Demand, sold his company to Salesforce after 15 years, despite previously resisting acquisition offers. The deal, which has an undisclosed sale price but is expected to generate US$75 million in revenue for Salesforce in 2023, was an "incredible opportunity" that Malpass felt accountable to his employees to pursue. Traction on Demand, a Salesforce partner with 1,500 global employees, will become part of Salesforce Professional Services, with Malpass focused on a smooth integration before exploring his next steps within the larger company.

feed.title: Disney ‘Taking Steps to Pause’ All Business in Russia
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company announced it is "taking steps to pause" all other businesses in Russia, beyond its initial halt on new movie releases, due to the "unprovoked invasion of Ukraine" and escalating humanitarian crisis. This includes content and product licensing, Disney Cruise Line activities, National Geographic magazine and tours, local content productions, and linear channels. Disney stated that while some activities can be paused immediately, others will take time due to contractual complexities, and staff in Russia will remain employed.

feed.title: Amazon announces 20-for-1 stock split
feed.summary: Amazon announced a 20-for-1 stock split, following similar moves by other major tech companies like Alphabet, Apple, and Tesla. The article explains that a stock split divides existing shares into multiple, smaller-priced shares without changing the overall value of an investor's holdings or the company's market capitalization. Stock splits are often done to increase a stock's liquidity and make it more accessible to a wider range of investors, though in an era of fractional shares, some of the practical impact is diminished.

feed.title: Amazon surges as stock split, buyback excite investors
feed.summary: Amazon's stock surged over 5% after the company announced a 20-for-1 stock split and a $10 billion share buyback, aiming to attract more investors and boost confidence. This move follows a period of lackluster performance for the e-commerce giant, despite its substantial growth since its IPO. The stock split, the first since 1999, and the buyback are seen as psychological boosts and a signal that management views the stock as undervalued.

feed.title: Autodesk Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
feed.summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) stock fell 3.74% on Friday, closing at $192.31, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also declined. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for the company, with its stock trading significantly below its 52-week high. Competitors like Apple, Adobe, and Synopsys also experienced declines, while Autodesk's trading volume exceeded its 50-day average.

feed.title: Autodesk Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
feed.summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) stock dropped 3.74% on Friday, closing at $192.31, underperforming the broader market which also saw declines with the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average falling. This marks the second consecutive day of losses for Autodesk, leaving it significantly below its 52-week high. The stock's performance was also mixed compared to competitors like Apple, Adobe, and Synopsys, all of which also experienced declines.

feed.title: IonQ to Present Research at American Physical Society (APS) Annual Meeting 2022
feed.summary: IonQ announced its participation in the American Physical Society (APS) March Meeting 2022, where co-founder and CTO Jungsang Kim will be honored for his pioneering contributions to quantum computing. IonQ researchers will present new advances in quantum chemistry simulations, risk aggregation using quantum generative modeling, and general quantum learning of joint probability distribution functions. The company will also discuss application-oriented performance benchmarks for quantum computing, including its new #AQ single-number benchmark.

feed.title: Metaverse Technology Redefining Digital Experience
feed.summary: The metaverse is emerging as a significant technological advancement, moving beyond gaming to redefine digital experiences across various industries. Companies like Facebook (Meta), Nike, and Microsoft are investing heavily, with projections indicating global metaverse revenue could approach $800 billion by 2024. While offering potential for retail, enterprise solutions, and new markets like virtual real estate and entertainment, the metaverse also faces challenges related to data security, legal frameworks, skilled resources, VR device limitations, and potential negative societal impacts.

feed.title: Netflix stock has now lost all its gains from the pandemic
feed.summary: Netflix's stock has lost all its gains from the pandemic, with shares falling to a 52-week low of around $332 and being more than 50% down from its mid-November high of $700.99. This decline is attributed to consumers returning to out-of-home entertainment, increased competition from other streaming platforms, and underwhelming subscriber growth forecasts, prompting Netflix to rethink its strategy and raise prices. Analyst Michael Pachter suggests that competition makes it harder to retain customers and increases content costs, while competitors are less likely to license content to Netflix now that they have their own platforms.

feed.title: TSMC’s largest customer makes up 26% of revenue
feed.summary: Sales from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's (TSMC) largest customer, believed to be Apple Inc., accounted for 26% of its total revenue last year, reaching NT$405.4 billion. Its second-largest customer, suspected to be Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), contributed 10% of sales. The US market remained TSMC's biggest buyer, while sales to China declined due to US sanctions against Huawei Technologies Co.

feed.title: Best AirPlay speakers in 2022: Apple, Sonos, Bose, and more
feed.summary: This article reviews the best AirPlay-compatible speakers available in 2022, featuring top brands like Apple, Sonos, and Bose. It categorizes speakers by their ideal use cases, such as all-around performance, TV integration, portability, and premium audio, helping users choose the best option for their iPhone, iPad, or Apple TV. Each recommendation includes pricing information and highlights key features like AirPlay 2 support, smart assistant integration, and sound quality.

feed.title: Apple’s Impact Accelerator unlocks new opportunities for US businesses to speed environmental progress
feed.summary: Apple has announced that applications are open for the second class of its Impact Accelerator, a program launched as part of its Racial Equity and Justice Initiative. The accelerator supports Black-, Hispanic/Latinx-, and Indigenous-owned businesses in green technology and clean energy sectors. The first cohort included companies like Rickman Enterprise Group, Diversified Chemical Technologies, Inc., and Argent Associates, which are now exploring partnerships within Apple's supply chain to advance environmental and social justice.

feed.title: The Best Yoga Apps for Stretching, Sweating and Even Sleeping
feed.summary: This article reviews the 12 best yoga apps available in 2022, categorizing them by their "superpowers" such as best for beginners, best for toning, or best for sleep. It provides details on each app's pros, cons, and unique features, helping users choose the best option for their fitness and wellness goals. The apps offer various styles of yoga, alongside other fitness modalities like HIIT and Pilates, catering to a wide range of needs.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs ActiveBeta World Low Vol Plus Equity ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:GLOV)
feed.summary: This article provides details on the Goldman Sachs ActiveBeta World Low Vol Plus Equity ETF (GLOV), including its stock price, key statistics, and holdings. GLOV invests in large- and mid-cap stocks in developed markets, focusing on value, momentum, quality, and low volatility factors. The ETF was launched on March 15, 2022, and is managed by Goldman Sachs.

feed.title: Apple Pay has arrived in Argentina: how to pay with the iPhone and what are its advantages?
feed.summary: Apple Pay is now available in Argentina, allowing iPhone and Apple Watch users to make contactless payments at merchants with NFC-enabled POS devices. The service works with Visa and Mastercard from several major banks like Patagonia, BBVA, Galicia, and ICBC, utilizing NFC technology for secure transactions. Apple emphasizes the security and privacy of Apple Pay, as it protects card numbers by assigning unique device account numbers and encrypting data, providing a safer alternative to physical cards and cash.

feed.title: Apple Pay, disponible en Argentina: estos son los bancos y las tarjetas con los que funciona
feed.summary: Apple Pay, the contactless payment system for iPhone and Apple Watch, has launched in Argentina and Peru. Several Argentine banks, including Galicia, BBVA, Brubank, ICBC, Macro, and Patagonia, along with Visa and Mastercard, support the service. Users can configure Apple Pay through the Wallet app and make secure, contactless payments using Face ID, Touch ID, or a passcode.

feed.title: Electronic Arts stops sales in Russia, bans Russian teams from esports events
feed.summary: Electronic Arts has ceased sales of its games, content, and virtual currency in Russia and Belarus in response to the ongoing conflict in Ukraine. The company has also removed Russian teams from its sports video games FIFA 22 and NHL 22, and banned teams from both Russia and Belarus from competing in official esports events. This action comes amidst a broader trend of companies, including Microsoft and CD Projekt Group, pulling out of the Russian market and following an appeal from Ukraine's deputy prime minister.

feed.title: Why Is Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-A, BRK-B) Stock Up Today?
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's Class A stock hit an all-time high, closing above $500,000 for the first time, buoyed by strategic investments in oil companies like Occidental Petroleum and Chevron. The holding company, led by Warren Buffett, also reported strong fourth-quarter earnings with a 45% year-over-year jump in operating earnings. This performance, along with its substantial stake in Apple, has pushed Berkshire's market capitalization above $730 billion, surpassing Meta Platforms.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway shares topped $500,000 each—here's why Warren Buffett says he'll never split the stock
feed.summary: Class A shares of Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently closed above $500,000 each for the first time, making it the sixth most valuable company in the U.S. Despite calls for a stock split to lower share prices, Buffett has consistently refused, arguing that the high price attracts sophisticated, long-term investors. He believes a split would lead to a less aligned shareholder base and offers no intrinsic value if not tied to company growth.

feed.title: Apple is rotten
feed.summary: The author criticizes Apple for what he perceives as predatory trademark litigation against small businesses and even a public school district. He argues that despite Apple's immense wealth, the company bullies smaller entities out of using generic terms or similar-looking logos, often forcing them to give up due to prohibitive legal costs. The article highlights several examples of what the author deems Apple's litigious overreach.

feed.title: LG's UltraFine 5K Display to return to take on Apple's Studio Display
feed.summary: LG's 27-inch UltraFine 5K external display, which was presumed discontinued after Apple unveiled its Studio Display, will return to the market next month. The display was only out of stock due to component shortages and high demand, not discontinued. LG expects the UltraFine 5K to be available again on its website and other retailers, offering a more affordable alternative to Apple's Studio Display.

feed.title: U.S. charges former Apple buyer with defrauding more than $10 million from company
feed.summary: U.S. prosecutors have charged Dhirendra Prasad, a former Apple Inc. employee, with defrauding the company out of over $10 million. Prasad, who worked as a buyer in Apple's global service supply chain, allegedly took kickbacks, stole parts using false repair orders, and caused Apple to pay for items it never received. He faces multiple criminal counts, including wire and mail fraud, money laundering, and tax evasion, with his arraignment scheduled for March 24.

feed.title: The BlackBerry veteran who took on Google and won
feed.summary: Patrick Spence, CEO of Sonos, a veteran of BlackBerry (formerly Research In Motion), successfully led Sonos in a patent infringement lawsuit against Google. The U.S. International Trade Commission ruled in favor of Sonos, affirming that Google infringed on five of Sonos's patents related to smart speaker technology. This victory demonstrates that smaller companies can win intellectual property disputes against large tech giants, emboldening Sonos and potentially setting the stage for future actions against other major players like Amazon.

feed.title: Apple, WiLAN settle patent disputes with license agreement
feed.summary: Canadian patent-licensing firm WiLAN Inc. announced a new license agreement with Apple Inc. relating to wireless technology, settling their patent disputes across the U.S., Canada, and Germany. This agreement comes weeks after Apple successfully appealed an $85 million court ruling in WiLAN's favor. The terms of the settlement were not disclosed.

feed.title: Vornado's Vision for the Future of New York's PENN District
feed.summary: Vornado Realty Trust is spearheading the transformation of New York City's PENN District, building on the success of the Moynihan Train Hall with significant public-private investment. The project focuses on creating an amenity-rich, post-pandemic office culture, attracting major tenants like Facebook and Apple, and revitalizing the area with new construction and updated iconic structures. This long-term vision aims to create a vibrant, livable city center and a comprehensive ecosystem for businesses and residents alike.

feed.title: How To Multiply The QQQ’s Dividend Yield By 15
feed.summary: This article criticizes the low dividend yield of Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and suggests an alternative, Nuveen Nasdaq 100 Dynamic Overwrite Fund (QQQX), for significantly higher dividend income. The author explains how QQQX uses covered call options to generate a 7.3% dividend, contrasting it with QQQ's 0.5% yield and the less effective Global X Nasdaq 100 Covered Call ETF (QYLD). It highlights QQQX's strategy of cherry-picking profitable tech stocks and its advantage as a closed-end fund often trading at a discount.

feed.title: Apple’s $4.7B in Green Bonds support innovative green technology
feed.summary: Apple's $4.7 billion in Green Bonds have been instrumental in fostering advancements in low-carbon manufacturing and recycling technologies, including a breakthrough in aluminum production that creates oxygen instead of greenhouse gases. The company plans to use this innovative direct carbon-free aluminum from ELYSIS in its iPhone SE. These investments are part of Apple's broader commitment to becoming carbon neutral across its supply chain by 2030 and expanding its use of clean energy globally.

feed.title: WT Dining Services Launches Mobile Ordering
feed.summary: WT Dining Services, in partnership with Aramark, has launched mobile ordering for Chick-fil-A, Which Wich, and Pony Express Burritos at West Texas A&M University. The new service, available through the Transact Mobile Ordering App, allows students, faculty, staff, and community members to order and pay using various methods, including Dining Dollars, Buff Cash, and credit/debit cards. This initiative aims to enhance customer service by providing a convenient way to access menu items.

feed.title: Perfect Corp.’s YouCam Apps Reach over 1 Billion Downloads Worldwide
feed.summary: Perfect Corp.'s YouCam Suite of Apps has achieved "Super App" status, surpassing 1 billion global app downloads. The suite, which includes YouCam Makeup, YouCam Perfect, YouCam Video, and YouCam Nail, leverages AI and AR technologies for beauty, photo effects, and video editing, generating 1.68 billion virtual try-ons monthly. The company attributes its success to best-in-class AI and AR innovations, fostering a loyal community that spends over 530 million minutes monthly within the YouCam universe.

feed.title: HP finance manager went on $5m personal spending spree with company card
feed.summary: Shelbee Szeto, a former HP finance planning manager, pleaded guilty to wire fraud, money laundering, and filing false tax returns after misappropriating approximately $5.2 million from the company. Szeto used company PCards to make payments to her own merchant accounts, then spent the funds on luxury items like Tesla and Porsche vehicles, high-end bags, and jewelry. Her sentencing is scheduled for July 20, 2022.

feed.title: Household Appliances Global Market Report 2022
feed.summary: The global household appliances market is projected to grow from $502.28 billion in 2021 to $557.70 billion in 2022, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.0%, driven by companies recovering from the COVID-19 impact. The market is expected to reach $818.87 billion by 2026. Key trends include the rising demand for outdoor cooking appliances and increased use of LED lighting in appliances for energy efficiency, while the growth of online on-demand laundry services may restrain the household washing machine market.

feed.title: How big U.S. companies are creating a brand new abortion benefit
feed.summary: Large U.S. companies are developing new benefits to cover travel costs for employees seeking abortions in other states, in response to stricter abortion laws being enacted across the country. Companies like Citigroup, Apple, Bumble, and Hewlett Packard Enterprise are offering such benefits, and some smaller companies like Alloy and Match Group are partnering with third parties to ensure access, privacy, and support for employees. This trend highlights the challenges employers face in navigating evolving legal landscapes, protecting employee privacy, and addressing legal actions, while also revealing disparities in benefit access for different worker groups.

feed.title: The SPY ETF Is an Attractive Long-Term Option to Buy in April
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) has seen an 8% decline since January but is up almost 5% in the last month, tracking broader market swings. The author suggests this dip, along with the recent interest rate hike, creates a good entry point for long-term investors. SPY, which tracks the S&P 500 and is the oldest and largest ETF in the US, offers diversified access to major companies, making it a reliable long-term investment despite potential short-term volatility.
